title: Database Access on ODBC
text: Database Access on ODBC Logon Path Specification The logon path for the OD x Connector is specified with the following assignment: $ODBC OD x : DataSourceName :|{ UserName }|{ Password } Copy The DBMS logon form appears when Uniface accesses the DBMS for the first time. The following information needs to be provided: Database— DataSourceName Identifier— UserName Password— Password Note: An ODBC database cannot be used for the Uniface Repository. Open/Close Support The open and close instructions are supported. The open instruction opens the relevant path and attempts to log on to the database. The close instruction closes all tables and logs off from the DBMS, shutting down that path. Related Links Database Access Uniface automatically opens the DBMS when a read or write ProcScript statement is issued. The path and logon details for the specific database (or databases) can be provided in an assignment file, a command line instruction, or in the open ProcScript instruction. Paths and Path Assignments A path is a named route to a resource required by the application, such as a database connector and database, a remote server, or a file location. Was this topic helpful?
